hero.headlineAfterAccent: systems you can run in production

slides.title: One engineer from UI to production APIs
slides.body: Ehsan owns the full delivery path: typed React and Next.js frontends, Node and Python services, PostgreSQL data models, and deployable releases. You get vertical slices you can demo, not handoffs that stall between teams.

slides.title: LLM features built with production guardrails
slides.body: Assistants, RAG pipelines, vector search, and tool calling are designed with grounding, fallbacks, logging, and cost control. The goal is reliable answers inside your business rules, not impressive demos that break under real usage.

slides.title: Architecture before hero features
slides.body: Every engagement starts with clear boundaries: API contracts, data ownership, failure modes, and observability. That discipline keeps velocity high without sacrificing operability when traffic, scope, or compliance pressure shows up.

slides.title: Scoped sprints, visible progress, on-time launches
slides.body: Ehsan works in async friendly cycles with written specs, working demos, and CI backed releases. Stakeholders see progress weekly, risks surface early, and MVPs reach production with monitoring instead of last minute fire drills.

slides.title: Backend, realtime, and integrations that hold up
slides.body: REST and GraphQL APIs, WebSockets, payment flows, calendar sync, and third party integrations are implemented as cohesive systems. Performance tuning, caching, and queue based processing are part of the solution, not afterthoughts.

slides.title: From prototype to governed AI workloads
slides.body: Document ingestion, embeddings, eval harnesses, admin tooling, and billing ready SaaS layers turn AI ideas into products teams can sell and support. Ehsan bridges research curiosity and the engineering rigor production teams expect.

trustStrip.label: Where solutions stay operational
trustStrip.lead: From schema design to guarded LLM workloads, disciplined handoffs teams can staff against.
